text: A single session of self-myofascial release with foam rollers significantly improves muscle flexibility in long-distance runners compared to no intervention.
Foam rolling acutely increases flexibility of the piriformis, tensor fasciae latae, and adductor muscles in long-distance runners.
Foam rolling acutely improves iliopsoas and rectus femoris flexibility in long-distance runners, as indicated by lower restriction values post-intervention.

Without foam rolling intervention, only the iliopsoas muscles showed significant flexibility improvement between repeated measurements, suggesting minimal test-retest learning effects for most muscle groups.
Self-myofascial release with foam rollers should be incorporated into the daily training routine of long-distance runners to maintain and improve muscle flexibility.
